author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Full DQ Agent (ideaslab-dq-agent) session. Fixed Lambda timeout on large datasets (removed 100K row cap, raised safety ceiling to 2M rows). Bumped Lambda memory from 1024MB to 3072MB via Terraform. Fixed two main branch issues: boto3 was in dev-only deps (not bundled in Lambda zip) and handler.py had a wrong _REPO_ROOT sys.path hack that resolved to /var/ instead of /var/task/. Fixed new-client crash: run_health_check.py crashed with FileNotFoundError when no config/clients/{slug}/field_map.json existed — added existence check with empty-dict fallback so pipeline runs for any client. Fixed CI: poetry.lock was out of sync after pyproject.toml changes, causing poetry export to fail; added poetry lock step in GitHub Actions workflow (two iterations: --no-update flag not supported in Poetry 2.x, then removed flag). All changes merged to ideasorg/main via PRs #29, #30, #31. Teams Copilot appPackage cleaned: deleted stale repairDeclarativeAgent.json, confirmed all files correct (manifest, dqDeclarativeAgent.json, ai-plugin.json, adaptive cards). Successfully ran full pipeline on 1,025,307 rows in 4m10s (2431MB RAM used, within 3072MB limit). Flow 2 ran successfully and saved outputs to SharePoint. Investigated Teams agent sharing: atk share fails with 403 for advanced declarative agents (Microsoft platform limitation — not tenant policy). Attempted fresh provision to bypass stuck title ID. Shared via sideload zip (appPackage.dev.zip) as workaround.
problems: Lambda timed out processing large datasets — 100K row hard cap was masking the real file size
boto3 was in dev-only Poetry group — not bundled in Lambda zip, causing ImportError in production
handler.py had _REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent which resolved to /var/ in Lambda (not /var/task/)
New clients without config/clients/{slug}/field_map.json crashed pipeline at Step 1 with FileNotFoundError
CI failed: poetry.lock out of sync after moving boto3 from dev to runtime deps in pyproject.toml
poetry lock --no-update not supported in Poetry 2.x — CI failed again on second attempt
atk share consistently fails 403 'Title not allowed to be modify' for advanced declarative agent
Fresh provision reused same title ID instead of creating new one — had to clear TEAMS_APP_ID and M365_TITLE_ID from env/.env.dev
After fresh provision, atk share still fails 403 — confirmed Microsoft platform limitation for advanced declarative agents
approaches: Changed nrows=100_000 to nrows=2_000_000 in run_health_check.py Step 0
Bumped Lambda memory from 1024 to 3072 in terraform/variables.tf and applied via terraform apply
Moved boto3 from [tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies] to [tool.poetry.dependencies] in pyproject.toml
Removed 3-line _REPO_ROOT sys.path block from src/handler.py — Lambda puts /var/task on sys.path automatically
Added field_map_path.exists() check in run_health_check.py — falls back to FIELD_MAP={}, OPT_FIELD_MAP={} with warning log
Added poetry lock step in CI before poetry export — first attempt used --no-update flag (Poetry 2.x incompatible)
Removed --no-update flag from poetry lock in CI — Poetry 2.x does not upgrade by default
Cleared TEAMS_APP_ID and M365_TITLE_ID from env/.env.dev to force fresh provision
Confirmed Microsoft docs: advanced declarative agents (with API plugins) cannot use atk share — scope must stay personal
Distributed appPackage.dev.zip (11KB, built 5/27 12:50) directly to teammates for manual sideload install
results: Full 1,025,307 row pipeline completed in 4m10s — 2431MB RAM, well within 15-min timeout and 3072MB limit
Lambda deployed successfully via GitHub Actions after poetry lock fix (PRs #29, #30, #31 merged to main)
New clients no longer crash — field_map fallback works, report runs with 'No match' for all IDeaS standard fields
Flow 2 confirmed successful: files saved to discovery_runs/data_health_reports/360chicago/outputs/2026-05-27T16-33-22Z/
appPackage cleaned: repairDeclarativeAgent.json deleted from both appPackage/ root and build artifacts
atk share permanently blocked for advanced declarative agents — platform limitation, not a config issue
Workaround: sideload zip sharing works; teammates install via Teams > Apps > Upload a custom app

decisions.decision: Remove 100K row cap; use 2M safety ceiling — full datasets must be processed
Lambda memory stays at 3072MB — 2431MB was used on 1M row run, no headroom to reduce
boto3 is a runtime dependency, not dev-only — must be in [tool.poetry.dependencies]
field_map.json is optional — new clients fall back to empty dicts, pipeline completes without crashing
Use poetry lock (no flags) in CI for Poetry 2.x compatibility
atk share not viable for this agent — distribute via sideload zip until Microsoft adds platform support
Teams notification on pipeline complete: post card to a fixed channel via Power Automate Flow 2 SuccessResponse branch
decisions.status: planned — not yet implemented in Flow 2

open_questions: Teams channel notification via Flow 2 not yet added — 'Post card in a chat or channel' action needs to be wired into SuccessResponse branch
Should field_map.json auto-generation be added for new clients based on dict.xlsx column names?
openpyxl is slow for large files (3m48s to load 1M rows) — is there a faster alternative (e.g. calamine engine via python-calamine)?
Flow 2 Teams notification: which channel should completion cards be posted to?
Can the DQ Agent eventually be published to the Teams org app catalog instead of sideload distribution?
